description: Per-platform SELECTED documented route with normalized transition counts and a unitless heuristic_effort_score. This is NOT a ranking, NOT observed time, NOT minutes, and NOT a vendor claim. 'Selected' means the record's committed route; documented alternatives were not compared under a shared measurement standard, so this is not an 'easiest path'. Canonical source: records/*.json.
caveats: heuristic_effort_score is a model output over normalized counts, not an observation. Trust ordering more than any absolute value.
Platform events are excluded from the score because they are automatic, not developer work.
Records are only conditionally comparable. Filter with ds-quality.json (comparability_status, starting_state, execution_environment) before comparing across platforms.
Re-researched records use a more compact step granularity than canonical records; see ds-quality.json re_researched.

model.heuristic_effort_score: weighted sum of normalized developer actions, documentation navigation, waits, and friction-gate weights; platform events excluded

rows.selected_surface: Bird Send your first email through deterministic sandbox delivery
rows.outcome: A deterministic sandbox email sent through Bird's shared onboarding domain is accepted and then reports delivered status without reaching a real mailbox.

rows.name: Coolify
rows.selected_surface: Web UI
rows.outcome: First application or service is deployed and accessible through Coolify with automatic SSL and domain configuration optionally set

rows.name: Mollie
rows.selected_surface: Payments API with Mollie Checkout
rows.outcome: Treat account and initial profile setup as one opaque step, then create a Checkout payment, process the webhook, and retrieve the payment to see status `paid`.

rows.name: MongoDB
rows.selected_surface: Create and configure an Atlas cluster with Atlas CLI
rows.outcome: The CLI prints the connection command and the documented cluster-created terminal signal.

rows.name: Postman
rows.selected_surface: Postman desktop quick start with Postman Echo
rows.outcome: The Postman desktop app sends a GET request to Postman Echo, displays the server response, saves the request in a new collection, runs a `Status code is 200` post-response test, and shows the test result.

rows.name: dbt Labs
rows.selected_surface: Local Mac Jaffle Shop DuckDB build
rows.outcome: dbt Core seeds the sample DuckDB database, creates the Jaffle Shop view and table models, runs the data tests, and completes all 28 nodes without warnings, errors, or skips in the guide's demonstrated terminal.

rows.name: Dagster
rows.selected_surface: Dagster OSS quickstart first local ETL pipeline
rows.outcome: The official local project materializes its `processed_data` ETL asset and writes the transformed sample rows, including the new `age_group` column, to `processed_data.csv`.

rows.name: HashiCorp Vault
rows.selected_surface: Developer quick start, Docker and Go path
rows.outcome: A local Go program stores the password value in Vault's version 2 key-value engine, retrieves it, confirms the value, and prints Access granted!.

rows.name: Porter
rows.selected_surface: Porter Web Dashboard and CLI
rows.outcome: Developer successfully deploys their application and accesses it live on a public URL

rows.name: Camunda
rows.selected_surface: Camunda 8 local Rocket Launch first BPMN process
rows.outcome: The supplied Rocket Launch BPMN process runs locally to successful completion with the documented `Odyssey` mission and `fuelLevel` of 95.

rows.name: Together AI
rows.selected_surface: Together Quickstart via cURL chat completions (prepaid credits)
rows.outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.together.ai/v1/chat/completions returns chat-completion model output.

rows.name: LangChain
rows.selected_surface: LangChain Python Quickstart, basic agent
rows.outcome: A LangChain agent using a selected chat model, weather function, and system prompt is invoked with the San Francisco weather question and prints its final content blocks after calling the tool.

rows.name: Vonage
rows.selected_surface: Send an SMS request with the Messages API
rows.outcome: The response is `Accepted` and includes `message_uuid`.

rows.name: Fivetran
rows.selected_surface: Fivetran free account trial first initial connection sync
rows.outcome: Fivetran has connected a selected source to a selected destination and completed the connection's initial data sync.

rows.name: Microsoft Dynamics 365
rows.selected_surface: Dynamics 365 Business Central AL HelloWorld get-started
rows.outcome: The generated HelloWorld extension compiles, publishes, and runs in the opened Business Central online sandbox session.

rows.name: Netlify
rows.selected_surface: Publish an existing folder through logged-in Netlify Drop
rows.outcome: Netlify has deployed the dropped project and the live site is available at its unique netlify.app URL.

rows.name: PlanetScale
rows.selected_surface: PlanetScale Postgres dashboard quickstart
rows.outcome: A PlanetScale Postgres database with a main branch is provisioned, and the developer has generated and recorded a default role plus a usable connection string.

rows.name: Anthropic
rows.selected_surface: Get started with Claude
rows.outcome: The developer has sent the quickstart's renewable-energy prompt to the Claude Messages API and received Claude's assistant message.

rows.name: Harness
rows.selected_surface: Harness CI first Cloud build pipeline
rows.outcome: A Cloud CI build for a selected Git branch executes the configured Run step containing `echo "Harness CI is fast"`.

rows.name: InfluxDB
rows.selected_surface: InfluxDB Cloud Serverless Free Plan through the InfluxDB UI
rows.outcome: The developer writes InfluxData's published home-sensor line protocol sample to the automatically created default bucket through the InfluxDB Cloud Serverless UI.

rows.name: WorkOS
rows.selected_surface: AuthKit AI Installer on a supported existing project
rows.outcome: The developer chooses a WorkOS product and route, configures the staging environment and credentials required by that route, executes its first workflow, and verifies the product-specific result.

rows.name: Intuit QuickBooks
rows.selected_surface: QuickBooks Online Accounting API CompanyInfo call through the OAuth 2.0 Playground
rows.outcome: The developer creates the required QuickBooks Online developer resources, uses the OAuth 2.0 Playground to obtain a temporary access token and sandbox realm ID, sends the documented GET CompanyInfo request, and receives a CompanyInfo object

rows.name: Redis
rows.selected_surface: Redis Cloud quick start
rows.outcome: A free Redis Cloud database is active and has been opened through one of the quick start's documented connection methods.

rows.name: SmartBear
rows.selected_surface: Swagger Studio email signup and first published API
rows.outcome: The candidate's sample OpenAPI 3.0 definition is published and its public Swagger Studio URL can be copied and shared.

rows.name: LaunchDarkly
rows.selected_surface: LaunchDarkly software-developer quickstart, SDK not selected
rows.outcome: In a selected SDK branch, an application initializes LaunchDarkly, evaluates a first flag for a context, and changes its visible behavior when the flag is turned on and off.

rows.name: Grafana Cloud
rows.selected_surface: Send Linux host metrics to a Grafana Cloud dashboard
rows.outcome: Recent metrics for the named host are visible in the dashboard.

rows.name: Microsoft Entra ID
rows.selected_surface: Register and run an ASP.NET Core web app with Microsoft sign-in
rows.outcome: The user is redirected back to the app after sign-in without an Entra error.

rows.name: Railway
rows.selected_surface: Fork and deploy Railway's prepared NextJS app from GitHub
rows.outcome: Railway has built the forked NextJS source, started its container, and marked the deployment Active on the Project Canvas with a reachable public URL.

rows.name: Zilliz Cloud
rows.selected_surface: Milvus Lite local Python quickstart
rows.outcome: Milvus Lite opens `milvus_demo.db`, creates `demo_collection`, and prepares three 768-dimensional entities for insertion.

rows.name: Esri Developer
rows.selected_surface: ArcGIS Location Platform API key and JavaScript Display a map
rows.outcome: In the JavaScript candidate, the CodePen application displays the topographic basemap for the Santa Monica Mountains and allows pan and zoom.

rows.name: CircleCI
rows.selected_surface: CircleCI Cloud Quickstart, GitHub App Create Project route
rows.outcome: CircleCI has created a project linked to the selected GitHub repository, committed or accepted a CircleCI configuration, triggered the first run, and displayed it as passing.

rows.name: Google Cloud Platform
rows.selected_surface: Deploy and open a sample Cloud Run container
rows.outcome: Create a project with billing, deploy the Cloud Run sample container, and open its public service URL to see the container's response.

rows.name: PayPal
rows.selected_surface: PayPal REST API get-started path through first authenticated sandbox API response
rows.outcome: PayPal's sandbox REST API accepts the bearer access token and returns an HTTP success response for GET /v1/invoicing/invoices.

rows.name: Spring Boot
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Spring Quickstart Guide: Hello World endpoint
rows.outcome: Generate a Spring Web project, add the documented /hello controller, run it with the generated Gradle wrapper, and receive Hello World from embedded Tomcat on localhost:8080.

rows.name: Klarna
rows.selected_surface: Klarna Payments playground web integration with the in-line widget
rows.outcome: A test customer completes the Klarna widget flow and the server exchanges the authorization token for a Klarna order.

rows.name: incident.io
rows.selected_surface: Slack signup, app installation, and first live incident
rows.outcome: A first live incident has been declared and incident.io has created its dedicated Slack incident channel and posted the default heads-up in the main incidents channel.

rows.name: Stream
rows.selected_surface: React Chat connected client with tutorial credentials
rows.outcome: The developer first chooses a Stream product and implementation route, then completes that route's distinct observable success.

rows.name: Resend
rows.selected_surface: Node.js SDK first transactional email
rows.outcome: After common key and domain setup, the developer uses a selected Resend quickstart to send a transactional email and receives that route's success response.

rows.name: Mapbox
rows.selected_surface: Render and interact with a Mapbox GL JS map using the CDN route
rows.outcome: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input.

rows.name: Clerk
rows.selected_surface: Use the Clerk CLI to scaffold Next.js and create the first signed-in user
rows.outcome: The profile icon is visible in place of Sign in and Sign up.

rows.name: Android Developers
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Create your first Android app
rows.outcome: Android Studio has built a Greeting Card Compose app whose Preview shows a personalized introduction with a selected background color and 24.dp padding.

rows.name: Flutter
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Set up and test drive Flutter
rows.outcome: The generated Flutter counter app runs in Chrome, retains its current counter state across a source edit, and uses the edited decrement behavior after stateful hot reload.

rows.name: CyberArk
rows.selected_surface: Conjur OSS Docker Compose store-and-retrieve demo
rows.outcome: The local BotApp authenticates to Conjur OSS, fetches the generated value stored in `BotApp/secretVar`, displays it, and reaches the quickstart congratulations message.

rows.name: Terraform
rows.selected_surface: Get Started - Docker: provision an nginx container with Terraform
rows.outcome: Install and verify Terraform, define a Docker provider, nginx image, and container, initialize and validate the configuration, approve terraform apply, and verify nginx at localhost:8000.

rows.name: Siemens
rows.selected_surface: Building X Accounts API sandbox, first UserGroups response
rows.outcome: The portal gets a developer to product and API selection, but each Siemens product defines its own access model and result. A complete Building X Accounts API sandbox candidate reaches a successful `GET me/usergroups` response listing the U

rows.name: React Native
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Recommended React Native framework path: Expo tutorial, Create your first app
rows.outcome: Create the StickerSmash Expo and React Native project, reset it to the tutorial baseline, run it on mobile and web, edit app/index.tsx with React Native core components and styles, and see the saved change applied to the running apps.

rows.name: Coinbase Developer Platform
rows.selected_surface: Create and verify a first EVM API Key Wallet account with Node TypeScript
rows.outcome: The terminal displays the Created EVM account label and a 0x-prefixed address.

rows.name: PagerDuty
rows.selected_surface: Trial Account Onboarding
rows.outcome: A new PagerDuty trial tenant exists with its first service, tested notification methods, the mobile-app step completed, optional schedule and integration choices resolved, and the onboarding flow has redirected the developer into the PagerD

rows.name: Workday
rows.selected_surface: Invite an Enterprise Developer Site user and connect App Builder to a Workday Development tenant
rows.outcome: The connection indicator is green.

rows.name: Marqeta
rows.selected_surface: Explicit Create sandbox, signed-in widgets, and simulation cURL
rows.outcome: The developer simulates the documented $10 VISA authorization in the public sandbox and can view that card transaction in Transaction Timeline.

rows.name: Twilio
rows.selected_surface: Send and receive a first Twilio SMS with Python and Virtual Phone
rows.outcome: The expected SMS text is visible as a received message.

rows.name: Cloudinary
rows.selected_surface: Email registration and Node.js backend SDK quick start
rows.outcome: Run a selected official SDK quick start end to end and observe its documented media output.

rows.name: Chargebee
rows.selected_surface: Skip optional AI population and use the Node SDK
rows.outcome: The test-site API response contains the created active subscription, its customer, the charged test card, and the generated paid invoice.

rows.name: FusionAuth
rows.selected_surface: 5 Minute Intro, Docker route
rows.outcome: The registered administrator logs into the sample Express application through FusionAuth and returns to the home page with a personalized greeting.

rows.name: Amazon Web Services
rows.selected_surface: Create a standalone AWS account and complete console orientation
rows.outcome: The developer reaches the documented Congratulations conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.

rows.name: Razorpay
rows.selected_surface: India unregistered-individual account to Web Standard Checkout test payment
rows.outcome: The developer completes one simulated Razorpay transaction using the product route selected for the business.

rows.name: Datadog
rows.selected_surface: Linux host Agent reporting via Fleet Automation
rows.outcome: Obtain Datadog organization access, select the correct Datadog site and Linux host platform, use Fleet Automation's in-app generated script to install and configure Agent 7, then verify an Agent metric with host and version metadata.

rows.name: ServiceNow
rows.selected_surface: Australia REST API Explorer tutorial, create and read an incident
rows.outcome: The developer opens REST API Explorer, sends a Table API v1 POST that creates an incident, captures the Location header's sys_id, sends the documented GET for that sys_id, and receives the inserted record.

rows.name: Boomi
rows.selected_surface: Data Integration Source to Target Flow quick start
rows.outcome: The developer creates reusable source and target connections, configures and saves a Source to Target Flow, runs it manually, and sees a successful run in the Activity panel.

rows.name: Auth0
rows.selected_surface: Vanilla JavaScript SPA login with manual Dashboard setup
rows.outcome: After account and tenant creation, the developer chooses an application architecture and framework, completes its Auth0 setup, and verifies that route's first authentication or authorization behavior.

rows.name: Zendesk
rows.selected_surface: Sunshine Platform: 10-minute Ticketing API quick start
rows.outcome: Use the JavaScript console inside a signed-in Zendesk Agent Workspace to execute the official loop of three Create Ticket API POST requests and verify the resulting Test ticket 1, Test ticket 2, and Test ticket 3 records.

rows.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
rows.selected_surface: Create an OCI Free Trial tenancy and connect to the first Linux instance
rows.outcome: The developer is connected to the default shell for the instance.

rows.name: Adyen
rows.selected_surface: Adyen online payments, Web Drop-in, Sessions flow, test environment
rows.outcome: The developer creates an Adyen test account and credentials, implements Web Drop-in using the Sessions flow, submits a documented test card payment, shows the authorized result to the shopper, and receives the matching AUTHORISATION webhook

rows.name: Salesforce
rows.selected_surface: Salesforce REST API Quick Start using Developer Edition, Salesforce CLI, and cURL
rows.outcome: The developer authenticates a free Salesforce Developer Edition org with Salesforce CLI, walks through the documented REST discovery and Account queries, patches one Account's BillingCity to Fremont, receives HTTP 204, and sees the changed

rows.name: Authorize.net
rows.selected_surface: Hello World Java sandbox charge
rows.outcome: The Java sample sends an authCaptureTransaction to the Authorize.net sandbox and receives a successful transaction response.

rows.name: 1Password Developer
rows.selected_surface: Mount and read a local .env file from a 1Password Environment
rows.outcome: After local app and CLI setup and creation of a 1Password Environment, the developer either reads mounted project secrets after biometric approval or completes an SSH and Git workflow with a 1Password-held key and device authentication.

rows.name: Cloudflare
rows.selected_surface: Workers CLI edited Hello Worker deployment
rows.outcome: The public workers.dev endpoint returns Hello Worker!.

rows.name: Honeycomb
rows.selected_surface: Ruby OpenTelemetry traces to a first Honeycomb trace view
rows.outcome: Choose US or EU, create a Honeycomb account and team, copy an ingest API key, instrument and run a Ruby service with OpenTelemetry, generate request traffic, query HEATMAP(duration_ms), and open one trace.

rows.name: Okta
rows.selected_surface: Create an Okta Integrator Free Plan org and complete an Angular SPA redirect sign-in
rows.outcome: The Profile view displays the user's name and the signed-in controls include Sign out.

rows.name: Qdrant Cloud
rows.selected_surface: Create a free Qdrant Cloud cluster and run the Python menu semantic search
rows.outcome: The developer loads the official menu-item dataset, embeds it with a free Qdrant Cloud Inference model, and performs the published semantic search for vegetarian dishes.

rows.name: BrowserStack
rows.selected_surface: Automate: run your first Cypress test (Kitchen Sink) on a public URL
rows.outcome: Obtain BrowserStack credentials, install the Cypress CLI, clone and configure the Kitchen Sink sample for the public app, execute it on BrowserStack, and inspect its cloud build and logs.

rows.name: Zoom
rows.selected_surface: Zoom General App creation build flow
rows.outcome: An authenticated Zoom REST API response returns the current user's profile JSON from GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/users/me.

rows.name: DigitalOcean
rows.selected_surface: Deploy a DigitalOcean sample directly from its template repository
rows.outcome: App Platform has automatically deployed the selected starter application and exposed it at the URL shown on the app Overview page.

rows.name: Neon
rows.selected_surface: Start with Neon, 1 - Basics
rows.outcome: A Neon project contains sample data, a development branch is modified and compared with production, then the development branch is reset so the two schemas are identical again.

rows.name: Couchbase
rows.selected_surface: Create a Capella free tier operational cluster and run Couchbase's first SQL++ query
rows.outcome: A Couchbase Capella free tier operational cluster exists with the included travel-sample bucket, and the developer executes Couchbase's documented SQL++ query against it.

rows.name: Agora
rows.selected_surface: Secured Web Video SDK Quickstart with two clients
rows.outcome: Two Web Video SDK clients have joined the same Agora channel with the same App ID, channel name, and temporary token, published their local audio and video, and displayed local and remote video.

rows.name: Vultr
rows.selected_surface: Deploy a Linux Cloud Compute instance in the Vultr Console and verify it over OpenSSH
rows.outcome: Vultr has provisioned the developer's first Cloud Compute instance, and the developer can open its Linux shell and verify the logged-in user.

rows.name: HubSpot
rows.selected_surface: Latest developer-platform Quickstart through local app-card preview
rows.outcome: The boilerplate app is built, deployed, installed, assigned to the default contact record view, served from local development, and visibly rendered on a contact.

rows.name: Discord
rows.selected_surface: Building your first Discord Bot
rows.outcome: A configured and installed Discord bot receives interactions through a public endpoint, runs the /challenge game across two users, and posts the calculated result to the original channel.

rows.name: Upsun
rows.selected_surface: Create a project and deploy local code with the Upsun CLI
rows.outcome: Upsun has accepted the committed configuration, built and deployed the application to the default production environment, and allows requests to the application's primary environment URL.

rows.name: Unity
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Unity Manual: Create your first project, using the Get Started with Unity learning template
rows.outcome: Create a Unity ID with a default organization and eligible subscription, install and sign in to Unity Hub, activate a license, install Unity Editor 6.1 or later, select the Get Started with Unity learning template, name and locate a project

rows.name: Scaleway
rows.selected_surface: Create a Linux CPU Instance in the Scaleway console and connect as root over SSH
rows.outcome: Scaleway has created the developer's first Linux CPU Instance, and the developer has authenticated as root through SSH with the uploaded Project key.

rows.name: Apple Developer
rows.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems
rows.selected_surface: Create and verify the beginner iOS SwiftUI Chat Prototype
rows.outcome: The live preview renders both exact messages as padded rounded bubbles, yellow first and teal second, matching the `hello-swiftui--second-bubble-modifiers-ContentView.swift` reference.

rows.name: GitLab
rows.selected_surface: Tutorial: Make your first Git commit
rows.outcome: Create a GitLab project, clone it over SSH, edit README.md on a branch, commit and push the change, merge it into the default branch, push, and verify the content and commit in GitLab.

rows.name: Akamai Connected Cloud
rows.selected_surface: Create a Distribution-based Linode in Cloud Manager and connect over SSH
rows.outcome: Akamai Cloud has provisioned and fully booted the first Linode, and the developer has connected to its Linux shell through SSH or the built-in Lish console.

rows.name: OVHcloud
rows.selected_surface: Create a public hourly Linux instance in the OVHcloud Control Panel and complete first SSH login
rows.outcome: OVHcloud reports the first Linux Public Cloud instance Enabled, and the developer has logged in through SSH as the image's preconfigured sudo user.
